title: Nomad Squadron

'''Nomad Squadron''' was a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Squadron/Legends|unit]] formed by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] following the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] in [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]]. Originally consisting of [[Alliance Underground Engineering]] [[RZ-1 A-wing interceptor/Legends|RZ-1 A-wing interceptors]], Nomad Squadron was commissioned as a reconnaissance and hit-and-fade squadron. The unit served both the Rebel Alliance and its successor state, the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], throughout the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. By the [[Battle of Helska IV|start]] of the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]] in [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], the Nomads were based off the [[MC80B Star Cruiser]] ''[[Allegiant]]'' in the [[Meridian sector/Legends|Meridian sector]] and were under the command of [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Tarrik (pilot)|Tarrik]]. There, the squadron patrolled the [[Sector/Legends|sector]] on alert for assaults from the [[Extragalactic/Legends|extra-galactic]] invaders.

Nomad Squadron's [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] were known for being cross-trained on virtually all starfighter classes employed by both the Rebel Alliance and New Republic. This was reflected in the unit's flight line in later years when it was not uncommon to see A-wings alongside [[Incom Corporation/Legends|Incom Corporation]] [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighters]] and [[Slayn & Korpil/Legends|Slayn & Korpil]] [[A/SF-01 B-wing starfighter/Legends|B-wing starfighters]].

==History==
Formed following the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] in [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], Nomad Squadron was one of the first full [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Squadron/Legends|squadrons]] commissioned by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]. Initially equipped with nimble [[Alliance Underground Engineering]] [[RZ-1 A-wing interceptor/Legends|RZ-1 A-wing interceptors]], Nomad Squadron's mission profile included reconnaissance and hit-and-fade assaults. However, as the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] progressed, Nomad Squadron's [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] were cross-trained on nearly every other starfighter class that the Rebel Alliance employed.<ref name="Wings" />

When the Rebel Alliance was reformed into the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], Nomad Squadron continued its service with the new [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] government. By the [[Battle of Helska IV|outbreak]] of the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]]<ref name="Wings" /> in [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]],<ref name="VP">''[[The New Jedi Order: Vector Prime]]''</ref> Nomad Squadron was under the command of [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Tarrik (pilot)|Tarrik]] and was stationed aboard the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] [[MC80B Star Cruiser]] ''[[Allegiant]]'' in the [[Meridian sector/Legends|Meridian sector]]. At this time, due to the squadron's reputation of being able to fly almost any starfighter, Nomad Squadron's flight line comprised not only A-wings, but [[Incom Corporation/Legends|Incom Corporation]] [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighters]] and [[Slayn & Korpil/Legends|Slayn & Korpil]] [[A/SF-01 B-wing starfighter/Legends|B-wing starfighters]] as well.<ref name="Wings" />

During the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] War, the [[New Republic Defense Force/Legends|New Republic military]] was initially slow to act to counter the [[Extragalactic/Legends|extra-galactic]] invaders. However, once the threat became clear, hundreds of [[Warship/Legends|warships]] were mobilized to defend the New Republic's borders. Nomad Squadron, along with the ''Allegiant'', patrolled the [[Hyperspace route/Legends|spacelanes]] of the Meridian [[Sector/Legends|sector]], vigilant for any signs of Yuuzhan Vong incursions.<ref name="Wings" />

Despite Nomad Squadron's status as one of the oldest squadrons to serve in either the Rebel Alliance or New Republic, it was not as well-known as units such as [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] and [[Wraith Squadron]].<ref name="Wings" />

==Organization==
Nomad Squadron were originally outfitted with A-wing starfighters, although extensive cross-training of the unit's pilots allowed them to fly virtually any starfighter deployed by the Rebel Alliance. In fact, the squadron's pilots considered it a point of pride and honor that they could fly almost any craft, at any time, for any mission. This cross-training served them well, and by 25 ABY, the unit was equipped with not only A-wings, but X-wings and B-wings, giving them versatility in combat. Under Captain Tarrik, the Nomads were considered to be an excellent combat unit and were capable of performing ground operations as well as [[Realspace/Legends|space]]-borne missions.<ref name="Wings" />

==Behind the scenes==
Nomad Squadron first appeared in ''[[On Wings of Rogues]]'', an article in [[Wizards of the Coast]]'s [[Star Wars Gamer 9|''Star Wars Gamer'' 9]] that was written by [[Jim Swallow]]. The article dealt with the creation of starfighter squadrons within a player's campaign, and Nomad Squadron was one of several example units detailed at the end of the article.<ref name="Wings" />
